text:
Program:News Specials

The American transgender swimmer Lia Thomas has lost a court case against swimming government body World Aquatics.

It means Thomas won't be competing in the Paris Games.

Back in January, Thomas took World Aquatics to the Court of Arbitration for Sport over its new rules which ban anyone who has undergone "any part of male puberty" from competing in the female category.

ABC NewsRadio's Sarah Dingle spoke with Dr Catherine Ordway - the Sport Integrity Research Lead at the University of Canberra.

The American transgender swimmer Lia Thomas has lost a court case against swimming government body World Aquatics, which means she won't be competing in the Paris Olympic Games.(ABC News: Kathleen Dyett)

title: Trans swimmer barred from competing in Paris Olympics - ABC listen
description: The American transgender swimmer Lia Thomas has lost a court case against swimming government body World Aquatics.
